content.text:
The bug has been fixed. Here's a summary of the change:

**Root cause:** `GeminiStreamingResponseBuilder.append()` at line 62 called `partialResponse.candidates().get(0)` without checking whether `candidates()` is null or empty. When the Gemini API returns a response with no candidates (e.g., safety filtering, quota errors, or malformed responses), this caused either a `NullPointerException` (null candidates) or `IndexOutOfBoundsException` (empty candidates list).

**Fix:** Added a null/empty check on `candidates` early in the `append()` method (lines 62-68). When candidates is null or empty, the method still updates the response ID, model name, and token usage (which may be available even without candidates), then returns an empty `TextAndTools` result gracefully. This is consistent with how the Vertex AI Gemini module's `StreamingChatResponseBuilder` handles the same case at lines 30-31.

result:
The bug has been fixed. Here's a summary of the change:

**Root cause:** `GeminiStreamingResponseBuilder.append()` at line 62 called `partialResponse.candidates().get(0)` without checking whether `candidates()` is null or empty. When the Gemini API returns a response with no candidates (e.g., safety filtering, quota errors, or malformed responses), this caused either a `NullPointerException` (null candidates) or `IndexOutOfBoundsException` (empty candidates list).

**Fix:** Added a null/empty check on `candidates` early in the `append()` method (lines 62-68). When candidates is null or empty, the method still updates the response ID, model name, and token usage (which may be available even without candidates), then returns an empty `TextAndTools` result gracefully. This is consistent with how the Vertex AI Gemini module's `StreamingChatResponseBuilder` handles the same case at lines 30-31.
